Exclude gitignored files from Lambda layer and source code bundles (#7)

* Initial plan

* feat: exclude gitignored files from lambda layer and code bundles

- Add gitignore_exclude_patterns() utility to bundler.py that reads the
  root .gitignore and returns all non-comment, non-blank patterns
- DepsBundler.__init__ now stores patterns; try_bundle passes them to
  shutil.copytree(ignore=...) so __pycache__, *.pyc, .venv/, etc. are
  excluded when copying workspace packages into the layer
- hello_stack.py passes exclude=gitignore_exclude_patterns() with
  ignore_mode=IgnoreMode.GIT to Code.from_asset for the lambda handler,
  keeping gitignored artefacts out of the deployment zip
- Add 5 new tests covering the new function and copytree ignore behaviour

Co-authored-by: klemjul <60812459+klemjul@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/klemjul/python-aws-monorepo-boilerplate/sessions/bbd1571f-8fa5-4142-b027-9555f94766b0

* fix: apply ignore callback at top level of workspace src copy

Replace the manual entry-by-entry loop with a single shutil.copytree call
on src_dir directly, so the ignore callback (built from .gitignore patterns)
is applied at every level including the top level of src/. The old loop
only passed ignore= when recursing into sub-directories, meaning top-level
__pycache__/, *.pyc, and similar entries would still be copied into the layer.

feat: configuration hot reload (#179)

* Initial plan

* feat(core): add ReloadableRoutingTable trait and hot-reload support to DynamicRoutingTable

Add a new `ReloadableRoutingTable` trait in `bitrouter-core` that allows
replacing the inner routing table at runtime. `DynamicRoutingTable` now
stores its inner table behind `Arc<RwLock<_>>` so it can be atomically
swapped via `reload()` while preserving dynamic routes.

Includes two new tests: `reload_replaces_inner_table` and
`reload_preserves_dynamic_routes`.

Co-authored-by: takasaki404 <193405421+takasaki404@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/bitrouter/bitrouter/sessions/19527a78-4d7e-4360-8197-2ab9f9232272

* feat(cli): add `bitrouter reload` command for configuration hot-reload

Add the `Reload` CLI command that sends a reload signal to a running
daemon. On Unix this uses SIGHUP; on Windows a flag file is written.

The server spawns a background reload listener that waits for the signal,
re-reads the configuration file from disk, and atomically swaps the inner
routing table via `ReloadableRoutingTable::reload()`. Dynamic routes are
preserved across reloads.

Key changes:
- `DaemonManager::reload()` sends the platform reload signal
- `AppRuntime::serve_with_reload()` wires the reload callback
- `ServerPlan::with_reload()` accepts a type-erased reload closure
- `shutdown_signal` now coexists with a `reload_listener` task
- Updated README with the new `reload` command

Co-authored-by: takasaki404 <193405421+takasaki404@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/bitrouter/bitrouter/sessions/19527a78-4d7e-4360-8197-2ab9f9232272

* fix(server): remove explicit `ref` in implicitly-borrowing pattern

Use `&paths` instead of `ref p` to fix compilation on Windows where
the newer Rust edition disallows explicit `ref` binding modifiers
when the match is already implicitly borrowing.

[code-simplifier] Simplify delayLoad by removing redundant state assignment (#18160)

## Code Simplification - March 24, 2026

This PR simplifies recently modified code to improve clarity and
maintainability while preserving all functionality.

### Files Simplified

-
`packages/dev/core/src/Materials/Textures/equiRectangularCubeTexture.ts`
- Eliminated redundant state assignment

### Improvements Made

#### Reduced Redundancy

The `delayLoadState` assignment to `Constants.DELAYLOADSTATE_LOADED` was
duplicated in both the `if` and `else` branches. Since both branches set
the same value, the assignment can be moved after the conditional block.
This eliminates code duplication while maintaining identical behavior.

**Before:**
````typescript
if (!this._texture) {
    this.delayLoadState = Constants.DELAYLOADSTATE_LOADED;
    this._loadImage(() => {
        this._loadTexture();
    }, this._onError);
} else {
    this.delayLoadState = Constants.DELAYLOADSTATE_LOADED;
````

Since serde-saphyr has no intermediate Value type, the env var
substitution pipeline now uses serde_json::Value as the intermediate
representation: YAML is parsed to serde_json::Value via serde_saphyr,
env vars are substituted in the JSON value tree, then the result is
deserialized via serde_json::from_value.

- Add generateCommitMessage() export: ports buildCommitMessage() logic
  from JS to Rust, removing the last JS function that read DiffReport data

  getAlsContent, and related helpers; retain only gitBin + git shell commands

- Remove buildCommitMessage() from main.ts
- Delete 9 orphaned types from types/index.ts (DecompressedAls, AlsChange,
  StructuralCompareResult, AudioTrackEntry, MidiTrackEntry, etc.)
- Uninstall fast-xml-parser (removes 68 packages)

JS is now purely transport: paths and buffers only. All ALS semantics
live in the Rust crate. One parser to update if Ableton's format changes.

commit.message:
fix: detect and adopt orphaned gateway on port conflict in cmd_serve

When the gateway PID file was stale/missing but a gateway process was
still listening on the port (e.g. after a crash or reboot), cmd_serve
would:
  1. Start a new Python gateway that immediately crashed (port in use)
  2. Poll the health check — which passed because the OLD gateway answered
  3. Show '✅ running' without a valid PID file
  4. Every subsequent ps/run/shutdown would see 'not running'

Fix: before starting a new gateway, check if the port is already serving.
  - If yes: use lsof to find the owning PID, write it to gateway.pid, and
    return 0 (adopt the orphan instead of trying to replace it).
  - If no: start fresh as before.
  - After health check, verify the PID file was actually written; if not,
    surface a clear error instead of silently succeeding with a stale state.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: write PID file after port bind; backend PID files for clean shutdown

Bug 1 - PID file race in gateway:
  serve() wrote the PID file BEFORE binding the port. If the port was
  already occupied (orphaned old gateway), the new process wrote a dead
  PID and crashed immediately. The health check succeeded because the old
  gateway answered, leaving a stale PID file pointing to a dead process.
  Every subsequent 'ps' or 'run' removed the stale file and said 'not running'.
  Fix: write PID file AFTER ThreadedServer() binds successfully.

Bug 2 - Orphaned backends survive gateway restart:
  When the gateway died without SIGTERM (OS kill, crash), its mlx_lm.server
  children became orphans. A new gateway could not register them and would
  try to start new instances on already-occupied ports.
  Fix: each ModelBackend writes its PID to ~/.mlx/backends/<port>.pid at
  start and removes it on clean stop. cmd_shutdown reads those files and
  kills each PID explicitly, even if the gateway PID file is gone.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
